label: UI gap discovery and authoring of PI-091/PI-092/PI-093 — three area-disjoint planning items to surface executive_summary in the desktop UI (create/edit dialogs + master panes) for planning_items, decisions, and sessions respectively. Discovery surfaced that PI-074 added the column to the data layer but no PI scoped the corresponding UI work. The three PIs split by entity-type rather than bundling into one because the file sets are disjoint (planning_items, decisions, sessions panels and dialogs each touch their own files), enabling parallel agent dispatch once the orchestrator (PI-081) lands.

session.title: UI gap discovery for executive_summary — authoring PI-091/PI-092/PI-093
session.conversation_reference: Claude Code session continuing the same terminal chat that produced SES-097 (PI-074 implementation). After SES-097 applied, Doug asked whether a follow-on PI existed to bring executive_summary into the desktop UI; investigation confirmed the gap and surfaced this session. CONV-068 captures the discovery and authoring.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "is there another PI to add the executive summary to the UI?"

1. UI gap survey. Queried the live API for all PIs mentioning executive_summary in title or description; results were PI-074 (resolved by SES-097), PI-075 (backfill, Open), and incidental mentions in PI-005, PI-010, PI-079. No PI scoped the desktop UI work. Surveyed WS-012's PIs (PI-074..PI-090); confirmed PI-075..PI-083 cover backfill + orchestrator infrastructure but skip the UI surface.

2. Existing UI surface inventory. Listed `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/dialogs/` and `panels/`; found six dialogs (decision_create, decision_edit, planning_item_create, planning_item_edit, session_create — no session_edit per DEC-013 append-only) plus three schema files (_decision_schema.py, _planning_item_schema.py, _session_schema.py) and three master panes (decisions.py, planning_items.py, sessions.py). Nine UI files total need to grow the new field.

3. Slicing decision. Considered one bundle PI (matches PI-074's shape of "all three entity types in one PI") versus three per-entity PIs. Picked three per-entity PIs: the file sets are disjoint, so once the orchestrator lands (PI-081) the three can be dispatched in parallel by independent agents without file-edit collisions. The trade-off — area=`v2-ui` will be the same on all three under PI-076's coarse-grained vocabulary so area-based parallel-dispatch would serialize them — is acceptable because the orchestrator can refine its disjoint-files check below area granularity when needed.

4. Drafted PI-091 (planning_items UI), PI-092 (decisions UI), PI-093 (sessions UI) following the Audience-facing-summary + Technical-scope pattern established by sibling PIs in WS-012.

5. Authored this close-out payload and the corresponding apply prompt.
session.summary: Identified a real gap: PI-074 added executive_summary to the data layer (migration, access, API, snapshot) but no PI scopes the corresponding desktop UI work. Authored three area-disjoint PIs (PI-091, PI-092, PI-093) — one per entity type — so the orchestrator (PI-081) can dispatch them in parallel once the file-disjoint check is reliable. Each PI covers its entity's create/edit dialogs and master pane row display. PIs are optional-required until PI-075 backfills and tightens to NOT NULL.
session.artifacts_produced: PI-091, PI-092, PI-093 records authored in this close-out payload. No code committed; no documentation files updated. This payload at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_098.json and its apply prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-098.md.
session.in_flight_at_end: PI-091/PI-092/PI-093 are Open at session close; none scheduled. PI-075 (backfill + NOT NULL) should tighten the create dialogs to required in its same close-out commit — the three new PIs are aware of this coupling and document it in their Technical scope text. Open question: PI-076's area vocabulary lists `v2-ui` as a single value, which means PI-091/092/093 share an area. The orchestrator's area-based parallel-dispatch would therefore serialize them. PI-081's design should specify whether the file-disjoint check operates within an area or only across areas. Captured as a sub-note inside each PI's Technical scope; not surfaced as a separate PI.
session.executive_summary: Surfaced and filed three follow-on planning items for the desktop UI work that PI-074 did not scope. PI-091 covers planning_items, PI-092 covers decisions, PI-093 covers sessions — each grows the entity's create/edit dialogs and its master pane to display and accept executive_summary. The three are split by entity type rather than bundled so an eventual orchestrator can dispatch them to parallel agents working on disjoint files. None of the three is scheduled at session close; PI-075's backfill should tighten the dialogs to required in its same close-out.

conversation.conversation_title: UI gap discovery for executive_summary + authoring PI-091/PI-092/PI-093
conversation.conversation_purpose: Determine whether a follow-on PI exists for the desktop UI surface of the executive_summary field, and if not, author the missing PI(s).
conversation.conversation_description: Single-conversation discussion within the same Claude Code terminal chat that produced SES-097. The earlier conversation (CONV-067) had resolved PI-074's data-layer scope and was already closed/applied. Doug asked whether a UI follow-on PI existed; a quick survey of the live PI list confirmed the gap. Discussion then weighed one-bundle-PI versus three-per-entity-PI scoping; settled on three per-entity PIs to enable parallel orchestrator dispatch once PI-081 lands. The three PIs were drafted following WS-012's Audience-facing-summary + Technical-scope template.

planning_items.title: Surface executive_summary in the planning_items desktop UI (create dialog, edit dialog, master pane)
planning_items.description:
Audience-facing summary: Brings the planning_items executive_summary field added in PI-074 into the desktop app for the planning_items entity. Today the field is read/writable on planning_items only via the REST API or apply_close_out.py; the desktop dialogs and master pane do not show or accept it. Without this PI, PMs and executives reviewing planning items in the app see no benefit from PI-074/PI-075's work for this entity type.

Technical scope: Edit `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/dialogs/_planning_item_schema.py` to add an `executive_summary` field entry with a multi-line `QTextEdit`-backed widget and a live character counter (`N / 800`, amber under 200, red over 800). Update `dialogs/planning_item_create.py` and `dialogs/planning_item_edit.py` to render the field and pre-submit-validate length 200-800 via the existing `validate_optional_length` access-layer helper (mirrored client-side so the inline error matches the API response). Add a third column or detail-row showing executive_summary in `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/panels/planning_items.py` — truncated to ~120 chars with full text on hover/expand. Field is optional until PI-075 backfills and tightens to NOT NULL; PI-075 then bumps the dialog to required and adds a required-asterisk to the label.

Dependencies and parallelization: blocked_by PI-074 (already resolved). Area=v2-ui under PI-076's vocabulary, same as PI-092 and PI-093. The three siblings touch disjoint file sets — PI-091 only touches planning_items-named UI files — so the orchestrator's file-disjoint check (a refinement on PI-081's coarse area check) can dispatch all three to parallel agents safely. Soft-coupled to PI-075 — PI-075's close-out commit should tighten this dialog to required in the same change set.
planning_items.executive_summary: Adds executive_summary as a 200-800 character optional field to the planning_items create and edit dialogs (with a live N/800 character counter) and to the planning_items master pane (as a truncated detail row). Wires the existing validate_optional_length access-layer helper into the dialog's pre-submit check so client errors match the API response. PI-075's backfill close-out will later tighten the dialog to required.

planning_items.title: Surface executive_summary in the sessions desktop UI (create dialog, master pane) — no edit dialog per DEC-013
planning_items.description:
Audience-facing summary: Brings the sessions executive_summary field added in PI-074 into the desktop app for the sessions entity. Today the field is read/writable on sessions only via the REST API or apply_close_out.py; the desktop create dialog and master pane do not show or accept it. Without this PI, PMs and executives reviewing sessions in the app see no benefit from PI-074/PI-075's work for this entity type. Note: sessions are append-only per DEC-013, so there is no session_edit dialog and no edit-time field-rendering work.

Technical scope: Edit `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/dialogs/_session_schema.py` to add an `executive_summary` field entry with a multi-line `QTextEdit`-backed widget and a live character counter (`N / 800`, amber under 200, red over 800). Update `dialogs/session_create.py` to render the field and pre-submit-validate length 200-800 via the existing `validate_optional_length` access-layer helper (mirrored client-side so the inline error matches the API response). Add a column or detail-row showing executive_summary in `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/panels/sessions.py` — truncated to ~120 chars with full text on hover/expand. Field is optional until PI-075 backfills and tightens to NOT NULL; PI-075 then bumps the dialog to required and adds a required-asterisk to the label.

Post-PI-073 note: when PI-073's Session/Conversation redesign lands and merges, sessions become stateful and editable through the new session lifecycle (planned → in_flight → complete). At that point a session_edit dialog will exist and this PI's scope expands to include it — but PI-073's close-out will own that follow-on work, not this PI.

Dependencies and parallelization: blocked_by PI-074 (already resolved). Area=v2-ui under PI-076's vocabulary, same as PI-091 and PI-092. The three siblings touch disjoint file sets — PI-093 only touches sessions-named UI files — so the orchestrator's file-disjoint check (a refinement on PI-081's coarse area check) can dispatch all three to parallel agents safely. Soft-coupled to PI-075 — PI-075's close-out commit should tighten this dialog to required in the same change set.
planning_items.executive_summary: Adds executive_summary as a 200-800 character optional field to the session create dialog (no edit dialog per DEC-013's append-only rule) with a live N/800 character counter and to the sessions master pane (as a truncated detail row). Wires the existing validate_optional_length access-layer helper into the dialog's pre-submit check. PI-075's backfill close-out will later tighten the dialog to required; PI-073's redesign will later add edit-time scope.
